title: pseudolysimachion commutatum
intro: Pseudolysimachion commutatum, also known as Alpine Whitlow-grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, where it grows in damp environments. Traditionally, Pseudolysimachion commutatum has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and bronchitis, among other health benefits.
